Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismiss Proposed Class Claims And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Nov-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mark Barton and Brendan Gannon of telephone number 415-441-5544 for Plaintiff Michael Bandindi.
Daniel McQueen of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP telephone number 213-620-1780 for Defendants Burke Williams, Inc. and BW Supplies, LLC.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, DEFENDANTS BURKE WILLIAMS, INC. AND BW SUPPLIES, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration is granted. The arbitration agreement exist and it covers all claims other than the PAGA claim. It is up to the Arbitrtor to decide all issues relating to the enforceability and scope of any class anction issues. Counsel for the Defendant is to prepare a form of order and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismiss Proposed Class Claims And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Continued To Nov-17-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorneys, Mark E. Burton and Brendan Gannon of the law firm Hersh and Hersh (415.441.5544) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Daniel J. McQueen of the law firm Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP (213.620.1780) appears as counsel for defendants/petitioning parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANTS BURKE WILLIAMS, INC., BW SUPPLIES, LLC'S Petition To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismiss Proposed Class Claims And Stay Proceedings is CONTINUED November 17, 2016 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed by October 27, 2016 and must be limited to the argument distinguishing Iskanian on the grounds of the language in the arbitration agreement and that, if this matter is to be sent to arbitration, it is the arbitrator who decides the class, the enforceability of class waiver issue. Supplemental reply papers are due November 7, 2016. Notice is waived.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
